Open a new Python environment or Google Colab notebook running Python 3.10
Install the latest keras package using pip: `pip install keras --upgrade`
Import keras from tensorflow with: `from tensorflow import keras`
Attempt to import `data_utils` from `keras.utils` using: `from keras.utils import data_utils`
Observe and capture the ImportError: `cannot import name 'data_utils' from 'keras.utils'`
Verify the keras version by printing `keras.__version__` to confirm it is a version higher than 2.12.0 (likely 2.13.0 or above)
Clone or download the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example code from https://keras.io/examples/generative/finetune_stable_diffusion/
Run the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example script as-is in this environment with the latest keras installed
Observe the same ImportError occurs during the import inside `keras_cv/models/weights.py` at the line `from keras.utils import data_utils`
Record the full stack trace and error message for validation
Uninstall keras and install keras version 2.12.0 using: `pip install keras==2.12.0`
Re-run the Fine-tuning Stable Diffusion example with keras 2.12.0 installed
Assert that the ImportError no longer occurs and the example starts training or runs further without the import failure
Print keras version again to verify it is 2.12.0
Document the reproduction steps and error message for confirmation
